  6. Key User Custom Fields in SAP S/4HANA SAP GUI UIs: How to control field properties (mandatory,...)
  7. Key User Custom Fields in Fiori UIs based on SEGW OData Services: How to control field properties
  8. Key User Custom Fields in Fiori UIs based on OData Services exposed with Service Binding
  9. Custom fields in S/4HANA: Key User versus Developer Extensibility
  10. SAP Screen Personas: How to position key user custom fields exactly where you want them to be

entry.title: SAP Commerce & Consumer Industries 2024 Year in Review
entry.content:
Reflecting on 2024: Another Landmark Year for SAP Commerce Cloud and Consumer Industries!

SAP has had a long and successful history of enabling businesses in consumer industries to navigate complexities and drive innovation.  2024 is no different and in many ways a milestone year!  SAP Commerce Cloud and many products that support Consumer Industries has been on fire, rolling out hundreds of cutting-edge capabilities, all designed to elevate customer experiences, streamline operations and delivering industry specific innovations for our customers.  

Curious? Keep reading to explore all the innovations and milestones we achieved!

SAP Commerce Cloud named Leader in Gartner Magic Quadrant for Digital Commerce for 10th year in a row

For the tenth consecutive year, SAP has proudly been named a Leader in the 2024 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Digital Commerce.  But there is more! SAP has also been recognized as a leader by IDC for both B2B and B2C Digital Commerce and honored with the TrustRadius Buyer’s Choice Award. While it’s always wonderful to receive such accolades, our true motivation lies in empowering our customers to drive profitable growth. Be sure to check out our customer stories, organized by industry, to see how businesses have realized financial and operational benefits with SAP Commerce Cloud.

SAP Commerce Cloud - Cyber Week Results

We had another spectacular Cyber Week to kick off the biggest shopping season. SAP Commerce Cloud:

  • Powered $12.2B in Gross Merchandise Value (GMV), a +49.5% year-over-year (YoY) increase
  • Drove +18% increase in average order value and processed +23.4% YoY increased order volume.
  • Delivered 59.3% YoY growth in GMV on Black Friday, 71.9% YoY growth in GMV over Cyber Weekend and 49.5% YoY growth in sales per minute We achieved these impressive results with 100% uptime!

Expanding Retail and Fashion Possibilities with S/4HANA

Building on our momentum, the SAP S/4HANA 2408 Cloud Release for Retail and Fashion showcases SAP’s dedication to delivering innovative, value-driven solutions. Focused on simplifying operations and addressing industry-specific challenges, this release empowers businesses to streamline their processes and scale globally.

With features like enhanced returns management and cross-docking optimization, retailers can reduce complexity and accelerate delivery times. Tailored innovations for the fashion industry, such as season determination enhancements, ensure that businesses stay aligned with customer demands. Additionally, the expansion into Switzerland, Portugal, Canada, and Spain extends our best practices to a broader global audience, supporting their journey toward operational excellence.

By integrating innovation, industry expertise, and global reach, SAP continues to redefine what’s possible in retail.

Retail Predictive Planning for a Rapidly Changing World

Fulfilling orders efficiently depends on having the right products available at the right time, and that’s where our Predictive Planning and Inventory Orchestration solutions shine. These tools integrate forecasting and replenishment logic directly into operations, ensuring that if demand shifts, inventory adapts. By tying into the SAP Customer Activity Repository, predictive planning solutions help unify commerce data and demand signals across channels.  Additionally, improved events management streamlines exceptions handling and planning schedules. By integrating predictive planning into daily operations, businesses can respond to demand shifts proactively, ensuring stock availability and reducing the risk of overstock or stockouts. We’re especially grateful to our co-innovation partners who have collaborated with us to achieve shared success and bring these capabilities to life.

Omnichannel Retail - A Foundation of Data and Insights through SAP Customer Activity Repository

SAP Customer Activity Repository, a powerful data, predictive planning, and omnichannel platform, ensures all operational data—from sales transactions and promotions to inventory levels and forecasts—received its regular update. We’ve focused on enhancing performance, adding a new promotion pricing service, and improving analytics across the board. Improvements in unified demand forecasting, and multichannel analytics contribute to a more holistic view of customer behavior and operational performance.

Consumer Products:  Drive Profitable growth through comprehensive Revenue Management

Great operations need to connect seamlessly with financial strategy, and that’s where SAP Revenue Growth Management (RGM)for Consumer-Packaged Goods (CPG) businesses comes into play. CPGs can now:

  • Easily define targets and KPIs for all your retail partners, ensuring a clear path to achieving sales objectives.
  • Streamlined Collaboration helps to break down organizational silos with seamless, cross-functional collaboration that keeps every stakeholder aligned.
  • API-First Integration: Integrate both in-house and partner solutions effortlessly, thanks to an API-first approach that simplifies connectivity and interoperability.
  • Efficient Spend-Activity Management: Quickly create, copy, manage, and approve spend activities while generating corresponding pricing conditions, ensuring a fast and accurate promotion cycle.
  • Unified Sales Planning: Plan both promoted and non-promoted sales within a single application, making it easier to maintain a holistic view of all your commercial activities.
  • Financial Execution with S/4HANA Cloud: Manage on-invoice and off-invoice liabilities directly through S/4HANA Cloud to maintain full financial control and clarity throughout the trade promotion process.

Built on SAP BTP, RGM leverages modern, cloud-native architecture to deliver scalability and flexibility, helping businesses adapt to fast-changing environments.

Because these features blend into existing finance and supply chain processes, decision-makers enjoy a clear, end-to-end picture of how their trade promotions influence the bottom line. Rapid spend-activity management, API-first integrations, and unified sales planning mean there’s no more guessing—just smart, data-driven action.

With RGM, businesses maximize promotion effectiveness, ensuring every dollar spent translates into measurable sales and margin growth.

From Financial Settlement to the Final Mile
After capturing new sales and securing growth, companies must accurately settle contracts, commissions, and payouts. Settlement Management/Condition Contract Management advancements let organizations set up internal commission agreements, track settled amounts and send documents automatically — streamlining the close-out process so teams can focus on strategy instead of paperwork.

Meanwhile, on the operational side, enhanced Last Mile Distribution (LMD) ensures that once goods are picked and packed, they reach customers smoothly. Updates this year brought better batch handling, refined authorization controls, and support for more traditional paper processes when needed. In a world where the last step often defines the customer’s lasting impression, LMD helps deliver on promises made at the start of the order cycle.

Going Global: Trade Management with Intelligence and Insight
For businesses operating across borders, complexity is the norm. Global Trade Management (GTM) integrates logistics, compliance, and financial requirements into a single streamlined process. This year, we focused heavily on analytics and building toward SAP Business AI capabilities. Consistent with the customer centricity of the other product areas, GTM teams continuously listened to customer feedback to inform priorities and future roadmap items. The result will be a more informed, more agile global trade operation—one that’s ready for the challenges of international commerce.

SAP Commerce Cloud: Continuous Innovation and New Capabilities

Throughout 2024, we continued to provide our customers with differentiating and innovative capabilities, enhancing our unified commerce solution. This year, through our Continuous Innovation release updates, we have introduced over 150 new capabilities. These enhancements span the core platform, Composable Storefront, Order Management Services, integration areas, AI tools, partner solutions, and more. We also finalized two new SaaS packaged business capabilities that will launch soon -- SAP Open Payment Framework and Search Service.

Let's get into some of the highlights!

Commerce Cloud Core Platform

We have added major enhancements to the core platform to improve the security and overall reliability.

  • Scalable sandboxes and consumption credits allows you to selectively ramp up or dial down specific services within non-production environments. SAP Commerce Cloud, consumption credits are the virtual currency (Capacity Units) that you use to self-scale with your non-production environments.

  • Canary deployment takes blue-green deployments a step further by routing a percentage of traffic to the new code version in a parallel production environment for up to 12 hours. This provides added confidence by giving you time to validate and ramp up gradually before going fully live. Canary deployment uses SAP Commerce Cloud, consumption credits.
  • Zone redundancy ensures that a disruption in one zone will not jeopardize your production environment. Already available to all at no cost, benefits include added resilience for your data centers, greater reliability for clusters, and increased availability for critical workloads such as search and storefront operations. Zone redundancy has been implemented for Azure SQL databases, the SAP Commerce Cloud application, along with key infrastructure, such as compute and load balancers.
  • Premium Disaster Recovery minimizes the data loss and amount of time to be back to regular online operations if a major disruption were to occur. It is now available for self-service purchase in SAP Commerce Cloud through your Consumption Credits.

Commerce Cloud Composable Storefront

We introduced new capabilities to our decoupled headless storefront that exclusively leverages SAP Commerce Cloud REST APIs. The updates include:

  • Assisted Service enhancements that provide customer service agents with a quick and efficient customer 360-degree view to provide exceptional service and problem resolution to their customers, and a quicker way to access customer order details.
  • B2B improvements that allow your customers to more easily track and edit quotes as well as configure complex Built-To-Order products with improved user experience that shows updated pricing and options in real-time.
  • Site security during customer registration with preconfigured support for Google reCAPTCHA v2. This added layer of security prevents brute-force attacks in registration and prevents bots from spamming websites.

Order Management Services
SAP Order Management Services unifies buying and fulfillment channels. The composable suite of cloud-native services enable distributed order management. We added many new features, including the following:

  • Workflow to automate the handling of exceptions. If an issue arises during the fulfillment request, the system can re-trigger the fulfillment in some problematic status and apply actions such as re-sourcing or sending a notification to the employee.
  • Live insights for on time performance that aggregates all the order data in a single place.

  • Workflow loops simplify orchestration by applying sophisticated workflows to an even broader set of order data with significantly fewer steps.
  • Branching nodes with a multitude of conditions allows for more control when orchestrating orders with deeper decision capabilities. This provides greater visibility into order fulfillment, allows responses to late customer change requests, and reduces returns by preventing unnecessary shipments.

  • SAP Order Management Sourcing and Availability added support for sets and bundles, the ability to review past sourcing information to improve operations in a unified commerce environment, and contextual updates to provide alternate sourcing results.

Comprehensive and Integrated SAP Suite

New integrations across the SAP portfolio optimize commerce operations, improve business workflows, and make sure you are creating seamless and exceptional customer experiences. Integration with SAP S/4HANA provides an undisputed advantage that unifies front and back-end processes to connect demand to supply and deliver engaging experiences across the customer lifecycle.

  • SAP S/4HANA Sales integration now provides flexible order adjustment capabilities and customers get automatically notified of order changes that are rejected by your ERP. These provide a single source of truth for orders, streamline order management and customer notification, and simplifies B2B order planning.
  • SAP Service Cloud integration improves customer retention and satisfaction by enabling SAP Composable Storefront users to request help and engage with customer service representatives by capturing and managing Service Tickets online.

  • SAP CPQ integration allows B2B storefront buyers to access documents with detailed specifications, price information, and terms and conditions leading to a better and more informed B2B purchasing experience.
  • Customer Data integration allows for a 360-degree view of the customer. This provides you with the customer insights needed to deliver highly personalized content and promotions.
  • Synchronous integration between SAP Commerce Cloud and SAP Order Management Services allows for faster and more efficient order processing, real-time order visibility and reduced overall operational costs.

For an in-depth look at the business advantages of using SAP Customer Experience solutions with SAP ERP, read the paper, Analyzing the Economic Benefits of Using AI-Powered SAP CX Solutions Natively Connected to SAP ERP.

Differentiated AI Capabilities through CX AI Toolkit

Artificial Intelligence/Generative AI was the hot topic this year and we delivered many new features that enhance the customer experience during product discovery and save your business users time through automated processes and workflows.

  • “Ask the product” feature allows your customers to ask specific product-related questions directly on the storefront and get the answers they are looking for through AI-generated replies based on product data managed in the backend. This could be anything from the fabric or cleaning instructions for clothing, to the color and brightness specifications of a light bulb, to the fit and sizing information of a shoe. Any information you have about a product can be made available.

  • Visual Search now goes beyond the retail industry cover all industries. Visual Search improves product discovery by delivering quick and accurate search results based on AI image detection. Let’s say you have an image of a nice dress that you are searching for. Simply upload it and let AI give you recommendations of either that exact dress or similar dresses that you might like. Product discovery has never been easier.
  • Product Image Designer lets you automatically create captivating new product images from simple text descriptions. The Background Editor allows you to transform or remove existing product image backgrounds, staying on-brand and boosting engagement. Imagine launching a new home décor product. How efficient would it be to have a background of a nice living room generated automatically from a text prompt, just as it would look like with a professional photo shoot? This is exactly the type of use case the Image Designer enables.

  • SAP Intelligent Product Recommendation is a cloud-native Business AI tool specially designed for manufacturers and distributors dealing with complex products. SAP Intelligent Product Recommendation uses historical data, paired with machine learning, to suggest the most suitable standard or customizable products, increasing sales and conversions.

Open Payment Framework (coming in Q1 2025)

SAP Commerce Cloud, open payment framework, provides a central payment "hub", where key Payment Service Providers, including Stripe, Adyen, and Worldpay have already been pre-integrated, so you do not need to worry about manual integrations anymore.

Organizations can easily adopt this plug-and-play solution, reducing integration complexity. The framework is extensible and headless, enabling independent operation of front and back ends, catering to diverse channel needs for global businesses, and adding innovative solutions seamlessly.

SAP Commerce Cloud, open payment framework, covers common payment requirements and end-to-end payment processes across authorization, capture, refunds, and re-authorization while automatically staying current with growing security and compliance standards. It also ensures customers have the freedom to pick their preferred payment method, which enhances the checkout experience.

Search Service (coming in Q1 2025)

At SAP, our goal is to provide our customers with all the flexible search and merchandising tools they need to enable intelligent product discovery, drive sales and increase conversions. And by modernizing our architecture ourselves, we are adhering to the same recommendations that we provide to our customers, that the move to independent cloud services managed by SAP is the best way forward to achieve a lower TCO, less development efforts and easier maintenance.

A key component of this strategy is our soon to be launched Search Service. It provides comprehensive search and merchandising features, including flexible search indexing & scoring, facets/synonyms/stemming/spelling checking capabilities with word suggestions, product variant grouping, and visual search bar configuration. It is provided as a SaaS service with 99.95% availability, a flexible API, automatic upgrades and out-of-the-box integration with SAP Commerce Cloud. It is ready for global expansion with multi-currency & multi-language support.​

And in combination with Intelligent Selling Services, we enable Intelligent Search, delivering highly relevant results to your customers with recent and trending search suggestions and using AI to boost the product results by individual interest and intent.​

SAP Recommerce
SAP Recommerce drives revenue and sustainability by capturing the second-hand online market for your products. We added many new enhancements this year to help manage the trade-ins.

  • Configurable product attributes: Admin users can create up to 15 customer-specific product attributes for trade-in items, so the application closely matches your business needs and displays the correct product details and attributes to customers on the storefront.
  • Item IDs: Workbench users can assign a distinct barcode or unique ID to a trade-in item. Users can view a page listing all trade-in items and filter by item’s barcode or unique ID value.
  • Sustainability data: We now provide clear visibility of sustainability data and impact for business users and customers with admins being able to import and view sustainability data for each SKU and search for products or metrics in the data.
  • Configurable workflows: We improved the usability and management of trade-in products through intuitive business tools and workflows. Admins can create custom workflows to suit specific programs, such as “clean only”, “clean and repair”, “repair only”. When the workbench user selects a condition indicator, the application generates the associated workflow steps, from “check-in” to “ready to restock”.

Telco and Utilities Accelerator
We bring more enhancements to this industry-specific capability to help organizations in the telco and utilities industries grow their revenue and transform faster.

  • Subscription services information. Customers can get a comprehensive understanding of their subscription services, for both fixed and personalized selections. Customers can also easily track modifications to their service contract selections, including pricing updates for greater transparency.
  • Support for utilities retail journeys from simple products to complex bundles.
  • New design of digital B2C energy sales in Telco and Utilities Accelerator Composable Storefront.
  • Improved integration with SAP S/4HANA for sales orders (utilities only)

Partner Solutions
Our growing partner ecosystem on the SAP Store adds to the already robust offering SAP Commerce Cloud through solution extensions.

  • Oneview Unified Commerce Platform extends SAP Order Management Services into the physical store to maximize profitability and improve customer outcomes. Oneview Unified Commerce Platform provides shopping convenience by supporting all in-store fulfillment types for web-based orders, and associate-optimized workflows allow for efficient picking and packing of orders and preparing them for customer pickup or direct shipment to the customer.
  • FrontNow is a virtual AI advisor that boosts customer engagement by providing your shoppers with personalized interactions. The AI advisor represents your brand in engaging, one-on-one conversations, and supports 85 languages for tailor-made shopping and service experiences.
  • ChannelEngine harnesses AI to optimize management across 950+ online sales channels and international marketplaces. By combining the strengths of SAP Order Management Services and ChannelEngine, you can operate commerce unbounded. You get centralized shopper order processing and access to rich insights into consumer demand, sales performance, and market trends.

This is just a snapshot of the many features we have launched this year. For comprehensive details, please refer to our documentation. 

Upcoming Events
Stay connected and learn more about the latest innovations by joining our upcoming webcasts:

  • SAP Commerce Cloud Q4 2024 Tech-Focused Update: January 14, 2025
  • SAP Commerce Cloud Q4 2024 Release Recap: January 22, 2025.

As we close this remarkable year, we wish you happy holidays and look forward to delivering even more exciting features in 2025!

Please share your feedback in a comment and follow SAP Commerce Cloud on SAP Community to connect and engage in the new year with fellow industry peers and e-commerce experts!

Looking Ahead: Confidence in 2025 and Beyond
The innovations delivered in 2024, from smarter order management to the latest S/4HANA release, demonstrate SAP’s unwavering commitment to helping businesses thrive in an ever-changing world. As we head into 2025, our focus remains on delivering features that drive value, ensure scalability, and create seamless customer experiences.

Together, we look forward to shaping the future of commerce and consumer industries!

Happy holidays and here’s to a fantastic year ahead!

entry.title: Re-initializing of deltas in BW4HANA from S4HANA (For error Subscription for DTP* deleted in source)
entry.content:
Overview:

Re-initializing deltas between S4HANA and BW4HANA is vital for maintaining precise data synchronization. This process entails removing the existing initialization request in BW4HANA, purging previous data from the data targets, and establishing a new initialization. The error message indicates that the subscription for the Data Transfer Process (DTP) has been removed in the source system (S4HANA).

To address this issue, examine the DTP settings in BW4HANA, recreate the subscription if necessary, review system logs for further details, and re-initialize the delta process.

Error Occurred in BW4HANA System:

Subscription for DTP* deleted in source system. The error is due to deletion on subscription in S4 side or the subscription of DTP is mapped to wrong source system.

 

Check ODQMON by putting data source name in queue and see the subscriptions. 

 

Solution to the error:

Step 1) To delete a subscription in T-Code ODQMON in S4HANA, open ODQMON, select the DataSource, go to the "Subscriptions" tab, and delete the desired subscription. Confirm the deletion by checking the "Queues" tab to ensure the request is inactive. 

 

Step 2) To initiate a new subscription from BW4HANA to S4HANA, it is essential to delete all existing requests in the target system. If these requests are not removed, you will encounter errors. Therefore, ensure that all pending requests are cleared before proceeding with the new subscription setup. This step is crucial to avoid any interruptions or issues during the process.

Open the monitor in SAP GUI

Change overall Status to RED 

And delete the requests one by one. Please ensure all the requests are deleted from the target before moving to next step.

Step 3) To begin, manually initiate the Data Transfer Process (DTP) in delta mode using Eclipse. After starting the DTP, check the ODQMON transaction in your S4HANA system to confirm that a new subscription has been created. This verification step is crucial to ensure that the data transfer setup is functioning correctly.

 

Note** If you continue to encounter errors on the BW4HANA side after completing the previous steps, please run the report RODPS_REPL_TEST in transaction SE38 for the data source on the S4HANA side. This report will help diagnose and resolve any remaining issues with the data transfer process.

entry.title: Application Jobs in On-Premise systems - part1
entry.content:
Classic batch jobs on the ABAP Application Server for S/4HANA Cloud and the BTP ABAP Environment have been replaced by application jobs. Application Jobs are a framework on top of the classic batch framework. How does it look like in On-Premise systems?

Related SAP FIORI Apps

1. Application Jobs - APP ID: F1240

    2023 FPS02 - https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F1240')/S29OP 

   This app enables you to schedule and oversee jobs related to applications. If you frequently perform manual tasks at specific times, the Application Jobs app can alleviate your workload by seamlessly executing these tasks in the background. It allows you to schedule routine jobs.

2. Application Jobs Templates - APP ID: F2058

    2023 FPS02 - https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2058')/S29OP  

   This app empowers you to construct your own application job templates. These templates are comprised of a parameter set that you can designate for the linked job catalog entry. The creation of multi-step templates, which can subsequently be launched via the Application Jobs app, is an essential function of this app.

3. Maintain Job User - APP ID: F6156

    2023 FPS02 -  https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F6156')/S29OP  

   This app allows you to modify the owner and user associated with application jobs that have been established from a job template. This feature can be particularly useful in certain situations. For example, when an individual transitions to a different department within the organization or departs from the company, necessitating the removal of the corresponding business user.

4. Application Logs - APP ID: F1487

   2023 FPS02 - https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F1487')/S29OP 

   You can use this app to display application logs. If you need to check at a glance if any errors occurred, this app gives you a clearly structured overview.

New Authorization Concept

You have access to the general Application Jobs App (Fiori ID: F1240) and wonder, why you don't see certain job templates or no job templates at all in the job scheduling dialog?

Application job introduces new authorization concept.

The new authorization concept can be activated using transaction SM30.
To do this, delete the content of the field CHECK_INACTIVE in the table USOBAUTHINACTIVE in transaction SM30 in the line with the following key:

PGMID = R3TR
OBJ_TYPE = SAJC

As in an On-Premise Software Product the end customer has the full control over system setup and system configuration he can decide at any time at free will to activate S_START or to not activate S_START. Therefore for SAP at no point in time it is transparent whether in a particular On-Premise system for the Application Job Framework the legacy authorization concept with S_BTCH_JOB and S_PROGRNAM is active and used or if the customer did activate the new authorization concept with the S_START authorizations.

For compatibility reasons, we recommend that you retain the authorizations with the old authorization objects S_PROGNAM and S_BTCH_JOB, since these are still used in classic SM36 jobs.

Application job requires new authorization concept with S_START, it means the respective customizing the usage of S_BTCH_JOB and S_PROGNAM is fully obsolete for Job Catalog Entries etc. in FIORI apps.

New Auth Concept

After the authorization objects assigned to the business users with S_APJ_TPL, then In the scheduling dialog of the  Application Jobs App you see only the job templates, for which you have the start authorization.
And The start authorizations for application jobs are only provided via application specific business catalogs. 

SAP Notes and Links

3363357 - The general Application Jobs App
3028505 - Application Jobs - New Authorization Concept
3190922 - New authorization concept for application jobs
3296779 - Display-only role for application jobs

entry.title: How to check if "Key User Extensibility" is available for a specific SAP FIORI App
entry.content:
Introduction :

In SAP, it's common to work on extensions or customizations for standard applications. This is where Key User Extensibility (KUE) comes in. It allows developers and key users to extend and customize SAP Fiori apps without needing deep coding skills.

But how can you check if Key User Extensibility is available for a specific app ? And if it is, how do you go about making those changes? In this blog, I’ll show you how to quickly check if Key User Extensibility can be used and guide you through the process of extending the app.

 

1. What is Key User Extensibility (KUE) in SAP?

Key User Extensibility (KUE) allows business users and developers to make light customizations to SAP Fiori apps without requiring deep technical skills or custom code development. However, before attempting any extensions, it's important to first verify if KUE is available for a specific app.

 

2. Steps to check if Key User Extensibility is available for a specific app

For this Demonstration, I have considered " Manage Sales order - Version 2" FIORI App ( App Id : F3893 )

Step-1 : Identify the SAP FIORI App Id

Using the SAP FIORI Launchpad navigate to the App that you want to extend.

Navigate to the User Profile Icon at the top-right corner of the screen, and select the "About" section.  This will give you the App Id.

 

Step-2 : Navigate to the App Documentation in the FIORI App Reference Library

Navigate to the SAP FIORI App Reference Library. Link : https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/

Search the App with the APP Id from Step-1.

This will open up the App Details.

Scroll down in the Product Feature Tab, where you will find a link to App Documentation.

Step-3 : Read the App Documentation

On clicking on the App Documentation it opens the App details in the SAP Help Portal.

There will be a tab for App Extensibility ( Key User ).

This section provides details about the Custom Field - Business Context for App Extensibility and any applicable Data Source Extension.

 

Step-4 : Implement Key User Extensibility - Custom Field Or Data Source Extension

 

Conclusion :

Finding the right SAP Fiori app that supports Key User Extensibility doesn’t have to be a challenge. With the steps outlined in this blog, you can quickly identify supported apps, verify their extensibility features, and start customizing them to meet your business needs.

entry.title: Billing relevance D in execution order items in Service with Advanced Execution
entry.content:
The concept of Billing Relevance in Service with Advanced Execution is already explained in a previous blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/billing-relevance-in-execution-order-item/ba-p/13574529

This blog is to showcase the Service with Advanced execution process flow with a specific type of billing relevance which is Billing on Completion (Key = D).

In SAP S/4HANA Service, "Billing on Completion" means that the billing process can start only once the service execution is completed, this means, when the status is set to “Completed”.  Typically, this process is started with the creation of a billing document request (BDR).

The quantity defined in a transaction, or the fixed price agreed for a service is billed once the transaction is completed.

The following steps depicts the process flow for Billing on Completion:

1. Create a service order and add an execution order item.

Service Order with an execution order item

2. Navigate to the item overview page in Edit mode, scroll down to the Billing assignment block and change Billing Relevance to Billing on Completion.

Billing Assignment block in execution order item

3. Release the item and save the service order.

Release execution order item

Maintenance order is generated, and the execution status of execution order item is set to Execution Planning.

Execution status after creation of maintenance order

4. Release the maintenance order after planning.

Releasing the maintenance order

5. Set the status of maintenance order to Technically Completed after the execution is complete.

Setting status to Technically Completed

This sets the execution order item status to Completed and execution status to Execution Done.

Execution status after technically completing the maintenance order

6. Open the Release for Billing app. Enter the service order ID and search. From the results, select the service order ID and then choose Release for Billing.

Once you choose Release for Billing, the service order disappears from the search results. In background, a Billing Document Request (BDR) is created corresponding to the service order item.

Release for Billing app

Back in the service order, the Transfer to Billing status is set to Successful. Also, the BDR number will be visible in Transaction History.

Billing assignment block

7. Open the Create Billing Documents app and search using the service order ID. Select the order from the search result and choose Create Billing Documents.

Create Billing Documents app

The BDRs are processed to create a customer invoice containing the details of the completed service. 

Invoice

The Invoice (Billing Document) is also updated in the transaction history of the service order and the Billing Status is set to Completely billed.              

Billing and Transaction History of execution order item after billing

8. Lastly, you can post the Billing Document to complete the process.

Post Billing Document

With this, we can understand how Billing on Completion works in service.

entry.title: Where can I learn the key process of SAP Treasury Management in SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Discovering SAP Treasury Management in SAP S/4HANA learning journey.

Overview
Upon completing this learning journey, business users, consultants, and other interested learners will gain a comprehensive understanding of SAP S/4HANA's full range of capabilities and business benefits for efficient Treasury operations. Building on this foundational knowledge, you will explore SAP's extensive integration capabilities with both SAP and non-SAP systems, the implementation strategy, and the key organizational changes that need to be addressed when adopting S/4HANA for Treasury management. Ultimately, you will be well-equipped to utilize S/4HANA's robust tools to enhance your organization's Treasury operations and financial management processes.

Learning objectives

  • Outline common challenges in Treasury, explain process improvements and efficiencies for users and organizations that result from implementing S/4HANA, such as streamlining the Treasury workflow
  • Discover key business benefits, use cases and scenarios for using Cash Management, Multi-Bank Connectivity, Treasury Risk Management, Market Data Management, Trading Platform integration and Advanced Payment Management in SAP S/4HANA
  • Explore SAP Business AI capabilities of Treasury in SAP S/4HANA

Prerequisites

  • A fundamental understanding of Treasury business processes

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 
I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Application Jobs in On-Premise systems - part3
entry.content:
For further study and own fun.

1. ADT to create class with Implementing the business logic.

 

Sample Code

CLASS zcl_test_apj_exe DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_apj_dt_exec_object.
    INTERFACES if_apj_rt_exec_object.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS zcl_test_apj_exe IMPLEMENTATION.
 --input parameters
  METHOD if_apj_dt_exec_object~get_parameters.

ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD if_apj_rt_exec_object~execute.

ENDMETHOD.

-- Getting the actual parameter values
-- Implement the job execution

ENDCLASS.
 CLASS zcl_apj_demo_class DEFINITION PUBLIC FINAL CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_apj_rt_run.
    INTERFACES if_apj_dt_defaults.

    TYPES:
      BEGIN OF ty_name_range,
        sign   TYPE c LENGTH 1,
        option TYPE c LENGTH 2,
        low    TYPE c LENGTH 50,
        high   TYPE c LENGTH 50,
      END OF ty_name_range.
    TYPES: ty_name_ranges TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_name_range WITH EMPTY KEY.

    "! <p class="shorttext synchronized" lang="en">Numbers available</p>
    DATA numbers_available TYPE abap_bool VALUE abap_true.
    "! <p class="shorttext synchronized" lang="en">Numbers</p>
    DATA numbers TYPE RANGE OF i.
    "! <p class="shorttext synchronized" lang="en">Names available</p>
    DATA names_available TYPE abap_bool VALUE abap_true.
    "! <p class="shorttext synchronized" lang="en">Names</p>
    DATA names TYPE ty_name_ranges.
    "! <p class="shorttext synchronized" lang="en">Some text</p>
    DATA text TYPE c LENGTH 255.
    "! <p class="shorttext synchronized" lang="en">Public attribute which is not used on the selection screen</p>
    DATA another_public_attribute TYPE c LENGTH 10.
ENDCLASS.

 

2. ADT to create job catalog entry as SAPJ/SE80 is no longer be used any more;

    Define header, section, group, parameters in ADT.

 

--Header Check class sample
CLASS zcl_test_apj_chk DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_apj_jt_check_20.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS zcl_test_apj_chk IMPLEMENTATION.
METHOD if_apj_jt_check_20~check_and_adjust.
ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

More Methods:

 

--Define potential search help in parameters

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED

@ObjectModel.dataCategory: #VALUE_HELP

@EndUserText.label: 'Test Plant CDS view'

@Search.searchable: true

define view entity zcl_test_apj_table 
as select from zcl_apj_table {...}

 

3. ADT to create job template as SAPJ/SE80 is no longer be used any more;

 

Sample Code
--https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/b5670aaaa2364a29935f40b16499972d/1f04ad22db0147b99ebc476708b749b6.html?version=202310.002

CLASS zcl_test_apj_simple_obj_gen DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_oo_adt_classrun.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS zcl_test_apj_simple_obj_gen IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD if_oo_adt_classrun~main.

    CONSTANTS lc_catalog_name      TYPE cl_apj_dt_create_content=>ty_catalog_name  VALUE 'ZTEST_MY_SIMPLE_JOB'.
    CONSTANTS lc_catalog_text      TYPE cl_apj_dt_create_content=>ty_text          VALUE 'My first simple application job'.
    CONSTANTS lc_class_name        TYPE cl_apj_dt_create_content=>ty_class_name    VALUE 'ZCL_TEST_APJ_SIMPLE'.

    CONSTANTS lc_template_name     TYPE cl_apj_dt_create_content=>ty_template_name VALUE 'ZTEST_MY_SIMPLE_JOB_TEMPL'.
    CONSTANTS lc_template_text     TYPE cl_apj_dt_create_content=>ty_text          VALUE 'My first simple job template'.

    CONSTANTS lc_transport_request TYPE cl_apj_dt_create_content=>ty_transport_request VALUE 'Y11K900361'.
    CONSTANTS lc_package           TYPE cl_apj_dt_create_content=>ty_package           VALUE 'Z_D028092_APJ_MAIN'.

    DATA(lo_dt) = cl_apj_dt_create_content=>get_instance( ).

    " Create job catalog entry (corresponds to the former report incl. selection parameters)
    " Provided implementation class iv_class_name shall implement two interfaces:
    " - if_apj_dt_exec_object to provide the definition of all supported selection parameters of the job
    "   (corresponds to the former report selection parameters) and to provide the actual default values
    " - if_apj_rt_exec_object to implement the job execution
    TRY.
        lo_dt->create_job_cat_entry(
            iv_catalog_name       = lc_catalog_name
            iv_class_name         = lc_class_name
            iv_text               = lc_catalog_text
            iv_catalog_entry_type = cl_apj_dt_create_content=>class_based
            iv_transport_request  = lc_transport_request
            iv_package            = lc_package
        ).
        out->write( |Job catalog entry created successfully| ).

      CATCH cx_apj_dt_content INTO DATA(lx_apj_dt_content).
        out->write( |Creation of job catalog entry failed: { lx_apj_dt_content->get_text( ) }| ).
    ENDTRY.

    " Create job template (corresponds to the former system selection variant) which is mandatory
    " to select the job later on in the Fiori app to schedule the job
    DATA lt_parameters TYPE if_apj_dt_exec_object=>tt_templ_val.

    NEW zcl_test_apj_simple( )->if_apj_dt_exec_object~get_parameters(
      IMPORTING
        et_parameter_val = lt_parameters
    ).

    TRY.
        lo_dt->create_job_template_entry(
            iv_template_name     = lc_template_name
            iv_catalog_name      = lc_catalog_name
            iv_text              = lc_template_text
            it_parameters        = lt_parameters
            iv_transport_request = lc_transport_request
            iv_package           = lc_package
        ).
        out->write( |Job template created successfully| ).

      CATCH cx_apj_dt_content INTO lx_apj_dt_content.
        out->write( |Creation of job template failed: { lx_apj_dt_content->get_text( ) }| ).
        RETURN.
    ENDTRY.

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

 

4. Setup authorizations 

Find out how to set up the authorizations for the Application Jobs app.

The authorizations for application jobs are the same as for classic batch jobs if you use the default concept. For more information, see 101146 Information published on SAP site.

You can switch to the new authorization concept for application jobs by following the description in 3190922 Information published on SAP site.

5. Maintain application jobs via a released API

   You can use the CL_APJ_RT_API class to maintain application jobs. You can do the following and more:

  • schedule an application job

  • retrieve the status of an application job

  • cancel an application job

  • delete an application job

6. Maintain application job logs

 

Sample Code
CLASS zcl_test_write DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_oo_adt_classrun.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS zcl_test_write IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD if_oo_adt_classrun~main.

    TRY.
        " Create a new Application Log
        DATA(l_log) = cl_bali_log=>create( ).

        " Add a header to the log
        l_log->set_header( header = cl_bali_header_setter=>create( object = 'ZOBJECT'
                                                                   subobject = 'ZSUBOBJECT'
                                                                   external_id = 'External ID' ) ).

        " Add a message as item to the log
        DATA(l_message) = cl_bali_message_setter=>create( severity = if_bali_constants=>c_severity_error
                                                          id = 'PO'
                                                          number = '000' ).
        l_log->add_item( item = l_message ).

        " Add a second message, this time from system fields SY-MSGID, ...
        MESSAGE ID 'ZTEST' TYPE 'S' NUMBER '058' INTO DATA(l_text).

        l_log->add_item( item = cl_bali_message_setter=>create_from_sy( ) ).

        " Add a free text to the log

        DATA(l_free_text) = cl_bali_free_text_setter=>create( severity = if_bali_constants=>c_severity_error
                                                              text = 'Some Error Text' ).

        l_log->add_item( item = l_free_text ).

        " Add an exception to the log
        DATA: i TYPE i.
        TRY.
            i = 1 / 0.
          CATCH cx_sy_zerodivide INTO DATA(l_ref).
        ENDTRY.

        DATA(l_exception) = cl_bali_exception_setter=>create( severity = if_bali_constants=>c_severity_error
                                                              exception = l_ref ).
        l_log->add_item( item = l_exception ).

        " Save the log into the database
        cl_bali_log_db=>get_instance( )->save_log( log = l_log ).

      CATCH cx_bali_runtime INTO DATA(l_runtime_exception).
        out->write( l_runtime_exception->get_text(  ) ).
    ENDTRY.

  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

7. Notification and exceptions

 

CLASS zcl_ntf DEFINITION PUBLIC FINAL CREATE PUBLIC.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES IF_APJ_RT_JOB_NOTIF_EXIT.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS zcl_ntf IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD if_apj_rt_job_notif_exit~notify_jt_start.
  ENDMETHOD.


  METHOD if_apj_rt_job_notif_exit~notify_jt_end.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

8. Define your own APP with report, setup sematic objects, mapping target etc.

 

Some others interesting ones:

What is the scope of the Application Job App F1240?
In Detail it was asked: Is it meant to show all the jobs which could be seen in SM37 or is there a limitation to the jobs which it can see?

Yes there is a limitation. As the name indicates the Fiori App F1240 will only display jobs of the type "Application Job" it will not display any other type of jobs. Therefore NO - F1240 will not show the same information like SM37. One major aspect for this is that F1240 will only depict the application jobs that are allowed to be seen by cloud customers and partners and will hide all SAP proprietary jobs which must not be exposed to cloud customers. SM37 by its architecture and nature can NOT differentiate such jobs and therefore will always show every job - event those that are SAP confidential and proprietary. Therefore this view will not be exposed in any cloud environment.

After adding Application "(F1204) Application Jobs" to a custom business role I cannot schedule my Application Job. Is this an error?
NO, most likely this behaviour is the correct and the expected one. F1240 is by default a READ-ONLY application that will only display all jobs of type "Application Job" scheduled in the current client by ALL users. By architecture the application does not deliver any S_START authorizations and therefore by default the scheduling of any application job is not possible. The application does offer a restriction type via which the customer administrator can maintain enhanced authorizations which allow the abortion of any application job of any user in the system. Example: maintaining restrictions with an asterisk "*" the Business User that will receive this particular customer Business Role will be allowed to abort every application job of ever business user in the system.

If you wonder, why in your particular system you just access for your testing/development this same application is assigned to a Business User - but this user is able to also create and schedule new application jobs, than the reasoning is in all likelihood the following:

if to a Business Role arbitrary other Business Catalogs / App Authorizations Variants are assigned the authorizations of these artefacts are also merged into the role
even though F1240 does not provide any S_START authorization → any other application F???? can contain S_START authorization
the user is able to schedule the jobs, because he received valid S_START authorizations via any arbitrary F????
I am working in a cloud environment (e.g. S/4 HANA Public Cloud, IBP, Steampunk...) and when I open SM37 I can see more jobs compared to application F1240. Is this an error?
NO. In cloud environments the access for customers and partners to particular content in transaction SM37 is prohibited and must not be shared with customer and partner users. SM37 is capable to display classic background jobs ([BC-SRV-BTC] SM36/ JOB_OPEN JOB_CLOSE interfaces ...), Technical Jobs ([BC-SRV-BTC-JR] SJOBREPO - strictly prohibited for external exposure, SAP proprietary jobs) and Application Jobs ([BC-SRV-APS-APJ] Reuse-UIs; F1240; external communication scenarios ...). These are three diverse frameworks with divers user groups and diverse use cases demanding diverse feature sets (even when they share some technological assets and the same DB persistence). Therefore the access to SM37 is solely granted to SAP employees and solely via SAP Support scenarios as classic SAP GUI transaction access.

I have scheduled a job via the interfaces JOB_OPEN / JOB_CLOSE and alike, but they do not show up in Fiori Application F1240 - Application Jobs. Is this an error?
NO. You have used the interfaces to schedule classic background jobs and not an application job. Classic Jobs are to be monitored in the transaction SM37. F1240 does only show Application Jobs and not classic background jobs.

Can I use any existing API which works on Background Jobs also with Application Jobs? - Example: BP_JOB_COPY / BP_JOB_CREATE / BP_JOB_MODIFY
It is not allowed to use any API that was designed for classic background processing (SM36/SM37) for application jobs (nor technical jobs). Application Jobs and Technical Jobs are designed as superior layer above the classic background processing and therefore are only allowed to be accessed via dedicated released APIs that take the new and independent framework specifics into account. Using other APIs can lead to error situations which are not covered by our support and development teams and can only be solved by removing such erroneous calls.

And funny questions:

How to delete application jobs in transaction SM37?
Check SAP Note: 3002849.
To enable this, the setting to delete Application Jobs must be activated in the system by using the report BTC_OPTIONS_SET, Option: Delete Application Jobs from SM37.Note: In SM37 application jobs appear under their technical job names. You can retrieve the technical name of an application job from the Job Scheduling Fiori App by adding the column 'Job ID' to the job overview.

 

 

 

Useful SAP Notes and links:

SAP Note 2829891 - Application Jobs in On Premise systems;

SAP Note 3373331 - Application Jobs in OnPremise - FAQ;

SAP Note 3441346 - Enable 'Maintain Job Users' app for OnPremise

SAP Note 3438433 - Show technical users in the 'Maintain Job Users' app

SAP Note 3002849 - Application jobs cannot be deleted in SM37

SAP Note 2832889 - Application jobs must not be changed in SM36 or SM37 Message no. BT895

Creating a Job Catalog Entry and a Job Template in ADT

Working with Application Job Catalog Entries | SAP Help Portal

ABAP Cloud Application Jobs: Overcoming Key Challe... - SAP Community

Classes and Interfaces of the Application Log API | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Application Jobs in On-Premise systems - part2
entry.content:
In 3442285 - Handling of hidden and read-only parameters in the Application Jobs framework

Hidden Parameters

Hidden parameters need to be handled in a special way. First it is not allowed to maintain any parameter values inside a Job Template. The reason is, that customers might create their own templates, either via Scheduling or via Template App, and there is no 'copy mechanism' for hidden parameters. Consequently the customer's template would not have any value for those hidden parameter. Secondly parameters values must be set in the special way:

if your check exit class implements old check exit interface IF_APJ_JT_CHECK the one and only place to maintain values for hidden parameters is CHECK_BEFORE_SCHEDULE method.
if your check exit class implements new check exit interface IF_APJ_JT_CHECK_20 the one and only place to maintain values for hidden parameters is ADJUST_HIDDEN method.
Read-only Parameters

Whatever holds for Hidden Parameters will also hold for Read-Only Parameters: The idea behind read-only parameters is that users are not able to change them. Even if they are displayed when selecting the corresponding template, they are neither send back from the UI (as this request could be manipulated) nor could they be set by the framework.

if your check exit class implements old check exit interface IF_APJ_JT_CHECK the only places to maintain values for read-only parameters are INITIALIZE.
if your check exit class implements new check exit interface IF_APJ_JT_CHECK_20 the one and only place to maintain values for read-only parameters is ADJUST_READ_ONLY method.

Solution

We recommend using the new check exit interface IF_APJ_JT_CHECK_20 as handling such parameters is easier.

It is also possible to use the GET_DYNAMIC_PROPERTIES method, which allows a parameter to be dynamically hidden or set to read-only. !! IMPORTANT!! Parameters marked as read-only or hidden in SAPJ cannot be made visible or editable using this method.

Now let us take one example to feel this from the pre-deliver job catalog entries - SAP_FIN_ICA_MATCHING_RUN_COMP which runs against on ABAP reports: 1. ICA_MATCHING_RUN_AFCA for accounting model(leading unit is RCOMP).

In SAPJ - Job Catalog Entry

Header

Parameters: P_AFC marked as hidden.

In SAPJ - Job Template Name

The standard report run as below and you can get that "Trigger from AFC" was ticked as default in the report.

The ABAP report has the following parameters:

  1. Matching Method (Mandatory);
  2. Fiscal Year:  It's part of Filter String in ICARM.  It's related to the Mandatory Filter Fields FISCYEARPER.
  3. Fiscal Period: It's part of Filter String in ICARM.  It's related to the Mandatory Filter Fields FISCYEARPER.
  4. Company:  It's part of Filter String in ICARM.  It's related to the leading unit.
  5. Data Cutoff (YYYYMMDDhhmmss); 
  6. Safety Interval (Seconds);
  7. Trigger Follow-Ups;
  8. Trigger from AFC; A technical field. Hidden in job template and AFC.

The check class CL_ICA_APJT_CHECK_COMPANY defined as:

In the definition of method: IF_APJ_JT_CHECK_20~ADJUST_HIDDEN, P_AFC will be hidden during job scheduling with the exit class.

Compared to the standard interface method:

After the authorization and permission assigned to your users:

Launch to the APP Schedule Matching Run Jobs By Company and then Choose Create to create a job based on the job template “Matching Run – By Company”.

Choose Step 2 Scheduling Options:

You can select the Start Immediately check box to trigger an immediate job run, or deselect it to define a future start date and time for a single run, or define the recurrence pattern for the jobs you want to run regularly.

Choose Step Parameters:
Select the matching Method you want to use, and optionally, enter Fiscal Year, Fiscal Period, Company, Data Cutoff and Safety Interval.
If you want the Follow-Ups to be triggered, you can tick the option ‘Trigger Follow-UPs’. And here you can see the "Trigger from AFC" as it was hidden in class method above.

After scheduling and job was completely, when you check the parameters, you will get from there that "Trigger from AFC" was hidden but actually send to job variables.

The end.

When you define your own job catalog entry and check exit class method, you can take this as a reference.

Thanks

-Eric

entry.title: Datasphere THE solution to maintain the business context & considerations for outbound data movement
entry.content:
Introduction

You probably have heard before that one of the strongest muscles that SAP Datasphere brings to the table is that it leverages, respects and maintains SAP business context from S/4HANA's semantic layer.

As Business Context I refer to: Metadata, Hierarchies, Currency Conversions, Unit of Measures, Multi-Language, Security Configurations; in other words, all objects associations that give business meaning to the data.

As an example: let's picture a simple yet common use case in supply chain analytics: you would like to understand the impact of discount negotiations you have had with your vendors/business partners in your procurement processes. Connections amongst your business partners (master data) and their related purchase orders, currency rates across countries (currency conversions), where they operate and in what levels, like international, national and regional geographic-wise (hierarchies) -just to mention few examples- act as the "glue" (business context) that gives business meaning on the "raw" data.

In my previous blog, I talk about the great flexibility that SAP Datasphere offers for data integration to enable Business Data Fabric Architectures. Especially when leveraging data federation mechanisms to reduce storage costs and copies of the data. However, it is known that depending on the use case and performance on the usage of high volumes of data, data replication might be the best option.

S/4HANA plays a key role in this journey, as the ERP Cloud core system, S/4HANA combines the power of transactions and analytical engine to expose enterprise data through virtual data models that are consumed with Core Data Service (CDS) views.

Additional note: SAP S/4HANA provides over 8000 CDS views where around 20% are delta capable. These CDS views are out-of-the box available for Data Extraction, and the ones that are not, can be data-extraction enabled by creating custom CDS views which retrieve the respective associations and semantic relationships they have with other views and objects.

Check this awesome blog from @FrankRiesner for more information about released data extraction enabled CDS views per S/4HANA version.

Let's see know why Datasphere is the spot on solution to maintain the business context of your SAP Data.

 

Semantic Onboarding - Leverage your SAP Data in Datasphere

Whether your SAP data consumption is through federation or replication, the Semantic Onboarding capability is an excellent way to provide visibility to technical and business data users of all released CDS views and their relationships/associations with other objects. The way that it works is that you actually import SAP semantics to Datasphere, enabling easy and earlier access to those views for data modeling and ultimately analytics consumption.

SAP Datasphere provides mechanisms to maintain the semantics of the SAP data for many business context objects such as the ones I mentioned above: Metadata, Hierarchies, Currency Conversions, Unit of Measures, Multi-Language, Security Configurations.

The Semantic Onboarding supports different source SAP systems, such as S/4HANA Cloud, S/4HANA, BW/4HANA, the BW Bridge and soon calculation views from HANA Cloud will also be supported, see the roadmap item.

Let's see a quick example of how the Semantic Onboarding looks and works. Here, I'm leveraging a connection to an S/4HANA Cloud system and importing the I_Product CDS View, to later see it available as a table in the Data Builder, where data analysts can start working on data models including material master information with all its associations: 

Semantic Onboarding: Searching I_Product CDS View for importing Material Master semantics

Semantic Onbarding: Preview of Dependent Entities of I_Product object

Data Builder: Review of I_Product Table and Associations with other views and objects pt 1

Data Builder: Review of I_Product Table and Associations with other views and objects pt 2

Data Builder: Data Lineage of I_Product on current usage in the Data Builder

Learn more about Semantic Onboarding here.

 

What about other SAP Cloud solutions? How does Datasphere leverage the Business Context of their data?

Even though this blog is focused to S/4HANA data, it is important for you to know that SAP Datasphere also provides Business Content to other SAP Cloud solutions. The Business Content are packages that includes pre-configurations to jump start analytics projects. Pre-configurations such as tables, views, facts models and analytics models, including SAP Analytics Cloud stories (prebuilt dashboards), among other data objects. These packages come out-of-the-box and you can import the ones you want in your Datasphere tenant.

Some of the packages include business content for SAP S/4HANA, SAP Ariba SAP Concur, SAP Signavio, SAP Success Factors, also for different Industries. Find more information here.

 

Need to Push SAP S/4HANA Data Out to a Hyperscaler Data Lake / 3rd Party Data System?

This can be a common scenario when you manage a big percentage of non-SAP data in your organization but still require certain SAP data to integrate with it. In the ever-growing data landscape, organizations may opt for 3rd party data lake solutions such as MS Azure, AWS, Google Cloud for this purpose.

In order to support customers in this journey, especially when the vast majority of data they are managing is not coming from SAP systems; SAP has launched a capability called "Premium Outbound Integration" to allow near real-time SAP data replication to 3rd party data solutions by using SAP Datasphere's Replication Flows.

SAP Datasphere's Replication Flows

*Restrictions to delta capture are listed here.

There are 2 main outbound replication options:

  1. It's inbound: therefore, I'm not explaining it further in this blog.
  2. Outbound data movement: Very handy when you want to leverage Datasphere's integration, modeling and data transformation capabilities on SAP data (and non-SAP data as well, depending on your use case), before replicating outputs to a 3rd party data system (like Azure as shown in the picture below)
  3. Pass-through option: Use Datasphere as a middleware to supported targets without any data transformation.

For more information about supported target systems, check the documentation here.

Important note: Datasphere provides the ability to replicate SAP data by leveraging ODP Data Replication APIs, leveraging Change Data Capture mechanisms from SAP systems such as S/4HANA, ECC and BW. SAP has an unpermitted usage of such ODP APIs for direct consumption in 3rd party systems, without an SAP solution such as Datasphere, 3rd party systems can consume SAP data through OData APIs, which may not be the best for consuming high volumes of SAP data. Learn more on this note.

 

Considerations of moving SAP data out

Although the Premium Outbound capability allows you to replicate SAP data out, there are some caveats you need to be aware when trying to preserve Business Context of the SAP data, being more specifically: from S/4HANA's CDS views.

Business Context like Currency Conversions, Unit of Measures, Multi-Language, Hierarchies and Security Configurations can be lost when replicated out, therefore you may need to do an extra work in your target data system to rebuild that context that has been lost.

 

  • Currency Conversions:

SAP Datasphere respects the relationships of the CDS views and its associations. SAP Datasphere applies currency conversion on the fly for local currency, company currency, global currency, etc. maintaining the business context of SAP data intact.

However, when the need is to replicate this SAP data out to a 3rd party data system (such as any of the ones mentioned above), without this enrichment you would need to select one currency of choice to export OR create a custom model for each currency converted amount in the target system. This can be cumbersome as you would need to process a higher number of measures and key figures.

For instance, when you have a dynamic nature of currency conversions (like in Datasphere) you can receive the amount on the needed currency converted amount. On the other hand, you would need to design the model with multiple columns (CAD, EUR, USD, etc). To mention a real-life scenario: when managing pricing condition tables, you'll need to multiply by the number of currencies you manage in your organization.

 

  • Unit of Measure Conversion

Similar to the Currency Conversions, SAP Datasphere has standard process to handle Unit of Measures (UoM) conversions, respecting the context coming from S/4HANA and making it easier to "translate" measures like (kg, gr, lbs, lt, gallons, etc.), especially when companies operates in multiple countries that have different measurement standards, requirements and specific company measures (like a palette of an specific product equals to 9 boxes in one country but in another equals to 12).

In the same way, when the need is to replicate this SAP data to a 3rd party data system, without the enrichment you would need to select one kind of UoM when exporting, and its business rules and context would need to be rebuilt in the 3rd party target system, potentially making it again a cumbersome process to deal with.

 

  • Multi-Language:

When leveraging SAP Datasphere for managing Analytics with SAP Data, the solution also provides the ability to pull up all the languages included in the source SAP system so that you can choose. It carries the texts of the master data in various languages. Take a look at this blog by @san_tran for an easy example.

On the other hand, when replicating the data out, you would need to select one language upfront (let's say English) and the rest of the context (texts in other languages) would need to be rebuilt in your target 3rd party data system. For this last, you would need to extract the text and the data and then separately extract every text description of your master data (for example material master) and then rejoin them again in your target 3rd party data system. This could be also cumbersome, however the option is available for use cases when it makes sense to move SAP data out to another non-SAP data system.

 

  • Hierarchies:

As SAP Datasphere leverages the Semantic Onboarding, this makes it so much easier to import semantics and associations, respecting business context of the data including, of course, hierarchies.

Similar to the previous examples, when replicating data out to a 3rd party data system, there is no accelerator to assembly hierarchies in the target system, therefore an extra and very cumbersome work would be needed to rebuilt hierarchies. Let's picture an automotive company scenario where you are dealing with millions of auto parts, multiple hierarchies including financial ones and versioning of those hierarchies. These could be expensive to maintain from scratch.

If your use case still needs to export SAP data out, take into consideration that you might need to flatten your hierarchies to become level in columns. Here users could only identify each level by their level name or number for drill down purposes (losing dynamic functionality as hierarchies might not be flexible anymore). If the wish is not to flatten hierarchies, then the rebuilt of those hierarchies would be needed to wiring them to your transaction data in the target system, this could represent higher costs as they can be multiple ones and maintenance of such hierarchies would be needed as well.

 

  • Security Configurations:

Data access controls are inherited from S/4HANA users into Datasphere. SAP BW/4HANA and SAP ECC with embedded BW authorizations can be imported into SAP Datasphere as well, making it easier to map and respect data access controls.

The approach leverages existing authorization objects from SAP S/4HANA to enforce row-level security in SAP Datasphere with Data Access Controls. Check this blog from @JensBraun to learn more about how to leverage S/4HANA authorizations into SAP Datasphere.

When replicating data out, all security configurations or data access controls get lost, leaving the unavoidable need to recreate security data access control in the 3rd party target data system.

 

Conclusions

Many organizations ask what the best solution is to manage SAP data for analytics purposes. SAP Datasphere is the spot on enterprise data warehouse solution in the cloud for this purpose. It respects and maintains the business associations of the SAP data, accelerating time to analytics with business context intact. Check my previous blog to revisit 10 reasons why SAP Datasphere makes sense for your data and analytics strategy beyond maintaining the business context of your data (like enabling a business data fabric architecture).

When managing SAP focused data use cases, the recommendation is to move forward with SAP Datasphere, leverage native connections and SAP business content. SAP Datasphere also provides multiple connectors to non-SAP sources, which means you can use Datasphere for combined enterprise-wide analytics.

Additional note: Recently, my team delivered a value case analysis based on the use of Datasphere to accelerate time to analytics while reusing and maintaining business context from S/4HANA and leveraging self-service tooling for data modeling in a Canadian Public Sector customer, resulting in a potential improvement in productivity of 50% in data modeling and in 1575 hours saved annually.

In addition, check this Gigaom field study on ROI on Datasphere, where they discovered that by using Datasphere there is an average of 72% accelerated data modeling and an approximate of 138% three-year TCO reduction vs a DIY implementation. Check the report here.

When does it make sense to replicate SAP data out? When you have other data use cases beyond SAP, or perhaps you have already invested in those non-SAP data cases with another technology, but you still want to use SAP-specific data and rebuilding the business context of the required SAP-specific data is not that cumbersome in terms of time and resource consumption in your organization.

I want to appreciate my data mentor Tony Cheung for his constant guidance which also made it possible for me to publish this blog.

 

Do you want to keep learning about SAP Datasphere?

SAP continues to add innovations into Datasphere and you can check them publicly in the roadmap.
SAP also continues to grow partnerships with category leaders such as Collibra, Databricks, Google Cloud, among others, to deliver even more effective, efficient and holistic enterprise data and analytics strategies for a wide range of organizations. 

Also, take a look at these learning journeys on the SAP Learning Portal.
